Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:02 ET
Dispersion trade signals flash as index puts collide with single-name call flow
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index P/C at 1.55 versus single-name P/C at 0.86 — widest defensive/offensive split since August unwind suggests institutional dispersion hedging.
  • 2. Implied correlation at 0.19 confirms stock-specific regime while $6.2B negative SPY gamma amplifies any directional moves into CPI Wednesday.
  • 3. Massive COIN call blocks targeting $170 strike through June signal crypto optimism despite broader defensive positioning.
NARRATIVE — defensive dispersion mode

The most striking signal today is the extreme divergence between index and single-name options flows — index P/C spiked to 1.55 (heavy put demand) while single-name P/C dropped to 0.86 (call-heavy), the widest defensive/offensive split in months. This pattern typically signals institutional dispersion trades where funds hedge portfolio exposure via index puts while expressing conviction in individual names through calls. The 0.19 implied correlation reading confirms this stock-picking environment, creating opportunities to be long single-name vol and short index vol as correlations break down. Dealer positioning amplifies every signal with SPY sitting at $-6.2B negative gamma — the largest short gamma position since the August volatility explosion. Any directional move gets magnified as dealers hedge their short options positions, making the Wednesday CPI print particularly dangerous. The gamma flip at $664.49 means any break above current levels around $658 triggers forced buying that could cascade higher. The most compelling single-name setup emerged in COIN with massive call blocks totaling over 24,000 contracts at the $170 strike across April and June expirations, totaling $40M+ in premium. This targets 27% upside from current levels and aligns with crypto's recent momentum, suggesting institutions are positioning for a sustained rally beyond near-term volatility. The June positioning indicates this isn't just an earnings play but a multi-month bullish thesis.

Layer 1: Sentiment -18Layer 2: GEX -$6.2BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.55 vs 0.86Layer 4: IC 0.19
